intent.primary_intent: find a wedding outfit that feels more occasion-appropriate than his usual workwear-based dressing

context.climate: cool damp autumn weather with likely indoor-outdoor transitions

analysis.why_this_works_now: He asked for a different style after noting his normal shirt-and-jacket approach feels too workwear-like at a wedding. The response shifts away from outerwear dominance and towards a refined knit-shirt base with cleaner trousers and polished boots, using texture, collar shape, and layering to make the look feel occasion-appropriate without becoming fussy.
analysis.persona_alignment: The outfit respects his preference for muted dark colours, structured shoulders, straight leg trousers, and weather-aware layering while solving the shirt issue by letting a knit or overshirt create the visual separation. It stays believable for someone who works in a museum, runs supplier errands, and needs to move comfortably through dinner and dancing.
analysis.prompt_alignment: The recommendation directly answers 'show me a different style' by offering an evening-wedding look that is cleaner and more occasion-appropriate than his usual approach, while staying true to his stated needs and observed wardrobe language.

recommendations.label: Dark green overshirt with charcoal knit for a wedding-smart casual
recommendations.logic_summary: This is the clearest different direction from his normal navy-jacket approach: a dark green overshirt gives occasion presence without feeling formal, and the charcoal knit keeps the upper body clean and polished when the outer layer is open over the shirt.

pieces.styling_note: Wear fully visible under the overshirt so the shirt collar and placket provide texture and polish without looking too dressed up.
Choose a smooth, non-sweat-out knit so the look reads intentional and easy rather than warm-only.
A trouser that visually matches the knit keeps the line clean and avoids the imbalance that cropped chinos can create.
This keeps the outfit grounded and wedding-appropriate while still matching his real-life needs.
Keep hardware minimal so the overall effect stays understated.

recommendations.label: Olive overshirt with charcoal fine knit and straight charcoal trousers
recommendations.logic_summary: This is the most reliable alternative if he wants to stay close to his usual palette but look distinctly more occasion-appropriate than a work overshirt alone.

pieces.styling_note: Let the shirt collar and placket show fully to create texture and polish without needing a blazer.
A smooth knit keeps the look refined and prevents the upper body from feeling boxy or too casual.
Matching knit and trouser tones keeps the line clean and avoids the imbalance that can happen with olive outerwear and darker trousers.
This keeps the outfit believable for a real wedding while still suiting his practical needs.
Choose very low-shine leather and minimal hardware so the outfit stays understated.

recommendations.label: Charcoal overshirt with dark navy fine-gauge knit and charcoal trousers
recommendations.logic_summary: This keeps him in familiar dark tones but changes the visual language by using a navy layer instead of the usual overshirt-navy combination, giving him more occasion presence without becoming formal.

pieces.styling_note: The shirt should stay visible and textured so the look reads occasion-appropriate rather than all-out overshirt.
This gives him the same tonal benefit as charcoal but with a slightly more occasion-ready contrast for a wedding.
Keep the trouser clean and straight so the navy-charcoal combination reads intentional rather than casual-workwear.
This keeps the outfit grounded and wedding-appropriate while still suiting his real-life needs.
Use minimal hardware and low shine to keep the look understated.

prompt: show me a different style

style:
  context:
    occasion: wedding

    logic_summary: This is the clearest different direction from his normal navy-jacket approach: a dark green overshirt gives occasion presence without feeling formal, and the charcoal knit keeps the upper body clean and polished when the outer layer is open over the shirt.
    pieces:
    - category: long-sleeve shirt
      color: soft ecru or washed ivory
      role: base layer
      styling_note: Wear fully visible under the overshirt so the shirt collar and placket provide texture and polish without looking too dressed up.
    - category: fine-gauge knit
      color: charcoal
      role: top layer
      styling_note: Choose a smooth, non-sweat-out knit so the look reads intentional and easy rather than warm-only.
    - category: trousers
      color: charcoal
      role: bottom
      styling_note: A trouser that visually matches the knit keeps the line clean and avoids the imbalance that cropped chinos can create.
    - category: derby-style boots
      color: dark brown or blackened brown
      role: footwear
      styling_note: This keeps the outfit grounded and wedding-appropriate while still matching his real-life needs.
    - category: belt
      color: black or dark brown
      role: accessory
      styling_note: Keep hardware minimal so the overall effect stays understated.

scene.setting: outdoor garden wedding reception with a polished formal-table setting and subtle rustic backdrop details
scene.lighting: soft evening natural moonlight mixed with warm indoor reception lighting, realistic low contrast, gentle skin tone rendering, matte fabric response
scene.mood: understated, mature, occasion-appropriate, calm and polished

pieces.description: soft ecru or washed ivory long-sleeve shirt with a clean placket, crisp cotton weave, visible collar and placket under the knit, worn fully open to the chest
charcoal fine-gauge knit, lightweight merino or fine cotton knit, matte finish, regular fit with enough room through the chest and upper arms, worn open over the shirt
charcoal trousers with a clean straight leg, mid-to-high rise, light-to-midweight fabric, hem sitting just above the shoe without a deep drop
dark brown matte leather derby-style boots with a rounded toe and stable sole, polished but not formal, suitable for uneven garden-ground footing
minimal dark brown or black belt with understated hardware

prompt.system: Create a photorealistic virtual try-on image of the same man in the provided photo context. Preserve identity, body shape, proportions, and observed fit behavior. Render the outfit accurately and believably, with realistic fabric drape, seam placement, and layering. Keep the result retailer-agnostic and editorially neutral. Avoid glamour styling, exaggerated poses, or fantasy lighting. The image should feel like a real wedding-casual outfit try-on taken outdoors in the evening, with subtle natural and reception light, mature color grading, and true-to-life texture. Use the photo-based observations: shirt and knit should sit cleanly at the shoulders and chest, upper body should not look bulky, avoid a cropped-chino imbalance, and keep the trouser line clean and proportionate. Prioritize understated occasion weight over formality or sportiness. Keep the palette restrained and mature: charcoal, soft ecru or washed ivory, dark brown. Show the man in a calm, polished pose that communicates he looks more occasion-appropriate than his usual workwear-based dressing.
prompt.user: I want to try a different style. I have a wedding to attend and I want to look more occasion-appropriate than my normal workwear approach. Render the top recommendation from the style intent: a charcoal fine-gauge knit worn open over a soft ecru or washed ivory long-sleeve shirt with visible collar and placket, charcoal trousers with a clean straight leg and mid-to-high rise, and dark brown derby-style boots. Keep the look understated, mature, and wedding-casual rather than formal. Use the photo observations to make the fit believable: the knit should sit flat over the shirt without pulling at the chest, the shoulders should align cleanly, the shirt collar and placket should add texture and polish from the front, and the trousers should avoid the cropped-chino imbalance seen in the photo. Keep the palette restrained and realistic, with matte fabrics and subtle evening light. The final image should feel like a real person photographed at an outdoor garden wedding reception, calm and polished, not dressed up, not sporty, not black-and-white, and not overly styled.
